title: NUMERICAL METHODS IN ENGINEERING ANALYSIS
what: Five MATLAB implementations of classical numerical methods applied to real engineering systems: Newton-Raphson (6-equation turbine cold-end), steepest-descent optimization (3-component air cooling, 9→2 constraints), RK-4 ODE solver (compressible duct flow, 3 phenomena), Forward Euler multi-body dynamics (3-mass shock damper, 300 s), and PID motor control (P / PI / PID comparison, −85→+85 RPM transition)

Each problem formulated to tight tolerances — turbine cold-end solved to 10⁻¹²% where standard Newton-Raphson diverged at 100,000 iterations; cooling optimizer converged in <1,000 iterations; RK-4 achieves O(h⁴) global error vs. O(h) for Forward Euler
how: Newton-Raphson: custom 3×3 Jacobian with 4-stage sequential decomposition (polynomial NR → direct calculation → scalar NR → algebraic) to isolate nonlinearity and eliminate oscillation; gradient descent with α=0.01, h=10⁻⁵ finite-difference gradient, 9 constraints algebraically collapsed to a single-variable objective before minimization

RK-4 integrates dM/dx across area change (de Laval geometry, α=0.25 cm/cm), friction (Fanno, f=0.005), and heat transfer (Rayleigh, β=50 J/cm) over 21 grid points in [0,5] cm; Forward Euler at Δt=0.1 s over 3,001 steps under sinusoidal (20 s period) + Gaussian pulse (t=50 s, σ≈3.87 s) forcing on m₁=108.9 kg

PID implemented with physical op-amp R/C network (Rp2=5,660 Ω, Ri=1,112 Ω, Ci=1 μF, Cd=0.1 μF → Kp=5.66, Ki=5,091.71 s⁻¹, Kd=2.83×10⁻⁴ s); P, PI, and PID all simulated on the same step input to isolate each mode's contribution to settling behavior
results: Turbine: CP=1.447 inHg, NKW=253,335.95 kW, NHR=7,973.97 BTU/kWh — all converged to 10⁻¹²%; cooling cost: $8,186.99 total (refrigeration $4,988.87, precooler −$1.64 net benefit via heat recovery, tower $3,199.76)

RK-4: subsonic M₀=0.7 decelerates to ~0.1 in diverging duct; supersonic M₀=1.5 accelerates to ~3.5 — opposing behavior confirmed; friction and heat addition both drive flow to M=1 (Fanno/Rayleigh choking) — directly applicable to rocket nozzle and scramjet design

Shock damper: minimum separation 3.087 m (collision prevention), max velocity 0.779 m/s in Mass 3 despite zero direct forcing (energy cascade via spring coupling), Mass 2 ±4 m range, steady-state onset ~100 s; PID outperformed P (persistent error) and PI (overshoot) for optimal step settling
